title: Total Sales were consistently higher in 2021 than in the same months of 2020
description: A clear upward time trend is visible in Total Sales when tracked by Invoice Date: every month in 2021 is higher than its matched month in 2020. This indicates a broad-based year-over-year revenue expansion rather than a one-off spike. Step 4 explicitly identifies this pattern across the full calendar year, and Step 3 supports it by noting that revenue is concentrated in larger records, amplifying the impact of the 2021 scale-up.

data_evidence.key_statistics: Step 4 reports that monthly Total Sales are much higher in every month of 2021 than the same month in 2020.
data_evidence.data_points: Matched-month comparison across 24 months shows a full-year year-over-year increase with no monthly exceptions mentioned.

title: Operating Profit followed the same strong year-over-year rise as sales
description: Operating Profit shows the same trend pattern as Total Sales, with monthly profit totals in 2021 running above the corresponding months in 2020. This suggests the business scaled profit alongside revenue rather than growing through low-margin sales alone. Step 4 states that Operating Profit sums are much higher in every month of 2021 than in 2020, while Step 3 shows a very strong Total Sales–Operating Profit correlation of 0.956, reinforcing the parallel movement.

data_evidence.key_statistics: Operating Profit sums are much higher in every month of 2021 than the same month in 2020; Step 3 correlation between Total Sales and Operating Profit is 0.9563074748281619.
data_evidence.data_points: All matched months in 2021 exceed 2020 profit levels, indicating sustained profit expansion over time.

title: Units Sold increased sharply in 2021 across the full year
description: Unit demand strengthened materially over time, with monthly Units Sold in 2021 exceeding the same months in 2020 throughout the year. This is a volume-led growth signal, not just a pricing effect. Step 4 identifies this as a consistent monthly pattern, and Step 3 shows Units Sold is strongly correlated with Total Sales at 0.913, indicating that rising volume was a major contributor to revenue growth.

data_evidence.key_statistics: Monthly Units Sold are much higher in every month of 2021 than the same month in 2020; Step 3 correlation between Units Sold and Total Sales is 0.9134314656070663.
data_evidence.data_points: The year-over-year increase holds across all matched months, pointing to a persistent demand expansion.

title: Transaction activity stepped up sharply from 2020 to 2021
description: The number of records rose sharply from 2020 into 2021 and then stayed elevated throughout 2021, indicating a structural increase in transaction activity. This matters because Step 4 notes that higher 2021 totals occurred alongside many more records, and not just larger average orders. The pattern helps explain why total volume and revenue grew even though average Units Sold per record were lower in 2021.

data_evidence.key_statistics: Monthly record counts rise sharply from 2020 to 2021 and remain consistently high throughout 2021.
data_evidence.data_points: The increase is described as a step-change between years rather than a gradual drift, followed by sustained high activity across 2021.

title: Average selling price fell to an early-2021 low before rising steadily through late 2021
description: Price per Unit shows a distinct trend over time: it is relatively higher in parts of 2020, drops to its lowest levels in early 2021, and then climbs steadily toward the end of 2021. This suggests pricing recovered later in the period after an early-year soft patch. Step 4 identifies this directional pattern, making it relevant for pricing strategy and promotion review.

data_evidence.key_statistics: Mean Price per Unit is lower in early 2021 than in 2020, then trends upward through late 2021.
data_evidence.data_points: The pattern spans at least 3 phases: relatively higher 2020 pricing, early-2021 trough, and sustained late-2021 recovery.

title: Online sales followed the broader 2021 revenue pattern with a summer build and year-end rebound
description: For the Online channel, Total Sales are expected to reflect the same 2021 temporal shape seen overall: lower levels in Q1, stronger performance through summer, softer fall results, and a rebound by December. This subspace is especially important because channel-level timing often differs operationally even when the aggregate trend is similar. Step 4 provides the overall 2021 sales path that this channel should be benchmarked against.

data_evidence.key_statistics: Overall 2021 Total Sales decline from January to March, rise into July, fall through October, and recover in November-December.
data_evidence.data_points: The global pattern contains 4 distinct phases in 2021 and is a useful benchmark for the Online segment.

title: West region profit should be monitored against the 2021 summer peak and fall dip pattern
description: In the West region, Operating Profit should be evaluated against the broader 2021 trend of early-year softness, summer strengthening, autumn decline, and December recovery. This is a valuable regional trend lens because Step 4 shows the business-wide profit cycle clearly, and Step 3 indicates profit is tightly linked to sales scale. Regional deviations from this shape could signal local execution or demand issues.

data_evidence.key_statistics: Overall 2021 Operating Profit declines in Q1, climbs into summer, weakens in fall, and rebounds strongly in December; Step 3 correlation between Total Sales and Operating Profit is 0.9563074748281619.
data_evidence.data_points: The reference pattern includes a trough in early spring, a summer high, a fall dip, and a December rebound.

title: Operating Profit has the most severe concentration of extreme values
description: Operating Profit shows 706 outlier records, representing 7.32% of the dataset, making it the most pronounced anomaly among the core financial measures reviewed. This indicates that profitability is heavily influenced by a relatively small set of unusually large or unusual transactions, which can materially distort average profit reporting and segment comparisons.

data_evidence.key_statistics: 706 outliers detected in Operating Profit; 7.32% of all records; severity marked high.
data_evidence.data_points: Operating Profit outlier rate (7.32%) exceeds Total Sales (6.77%), Units Sold (5.27%), Price per Unit (0.84%), and Operating Margin (0.46%).

title: Revenue is distorted by a large tail of extreme Total Sales records
description: Total Sales contains 653 outlier records, or 6.77% of all observations, confirming a substantial set of anomalously large revenue transactions. Combined with the strong right skew noted in the statistical review, this suggests that a small minority of records contributes a disproportionate share of revenue and can bias topline KPIs upward.

data_evidence.key_statistics: 653 outliers detected in Total Sales; 6.77% of records; severity marked high.
data_evidence.data_points: Step 3 also notes extreme right skew and a large mean-median gap for Total Sales, reinforcing that these are not isolated anomalies but part of a concentrated upper tail.

title: Units Sold includes a meaningful cluster of unusually large-volume transactions
description: Units Sold has 508 outlier records, equal to 5.27% of the dataset, indicating that demand volume is punctuated by a notable number of exceptionally large orders or potentially erroneous quantities. Because Units Sold is strongly correlated with Total Sales (0.913) and Operating Profit (0.892), these volume anomalies likely propagate directly into revenue and profit outliers.

data_evidence.key_statistics: 508 outliers in Units Sold; 5.27% of records.
data_evidence.data_points: Step 3 correlations: Units Sold vs Total Sales = 0.9134314656070663; Units Sold vs Operating Profit = 0.8923794236295555.

title: Price per Unit anomalies are rare but still material for pricing integrity
description: Only 81 records, or 0.84% of the dataset, are outliers in Price per Unit, which is far lower than the anomaly rates for sales, profit, and volume. Even so, because pricing directly affects Total Sales and margin calculations, this small set of price anomalies could still explain some extreme revenue or profit records and should be reviewed as high-impact exceptions rather than ignored.

data_evidence.key_statistics: 81 outliers in Price per Unit; 0.84% of records; severity marked medium.
data_evidence.data_points: The outlier rate is much lower than Operating Profit (7.32%), Total Sales (6.77%), and Units Sold (5.27%), suggesting fewer but potentially influential pricing exceptions.

title: Operating Margin is unusually stable despite large anomalies in dollar metrics
description: Operating Margin has just 44 outlier records, or 0.46% of observations, compared with 706 for Operating Profit and 653 for Total Sales. This sharp contrast indicates that the main anomalies are driven more by transaction size and volume than by unusual percentage profitability, making margin comparatively stable even when dollar values are extreme.

data_evidence.key_statistics: Operating Margin outliers = 44 records (0.46%) from Step 2; Step 3 states Operating Margin is much more stable than dollar-based measures.
data_evidence.data_points: Operating Margin outlier rate (0.46%) is dramatically below Operating Profit (7.32%) and Total Sales (6.77%).

title: West region anomalies are likely to be amplified by the dataset-wide 2021 transaction surge
description: For the West subspace, anomaly risk should be interpreted in the context of the broader dataset shift: Step 4 reports a sharp year-over-year step-up in transaction volume in 2021, while Step 2 shows 653 Total Sales outliers and 706 Operating Profit outliers overall. In a high-volume region such as West, this kind of structural jump can make extreme records appear more frequently simply because there are many more transactions, so outlier review should separate true exceptions from scale effects.

data_evidence.key_statistics: Step 4 identifies a sharp year-over-year step-up in transaction volume in 2021; Step 2 reports 653 Total Sales outliers and 706 Operating Profit outliers overall.
data_evidence.data_points: The anomaly context is shaped by both higher 2021 record counts and elevated outlier counts in the main financial measures.

title: Online sales deserve targeted anomaly review because small pricing errors can cascade into large financial outliers
description: Within the Online channel, the dataset-wide pattern suggests a concentrated anomaly risk: 81 Price per Unit outliers (0.84%), 653 Total Sales outliers (6.77%), and 706 Operating Profit outliers (7.32%). Since Step 3 shows strong structural links from Units Sold to Total Sales (0.913) and from Total Sales to Operating Profit (0.956), even a limited number of unusual online prices or order sizes could produce disproportionately large downstream financial anomalies.

data_evidence.key_statistics: Correlations: Units Sold vs Total Sales = 0.9134314656070663; Total Sales vs Operating Profit = 0.9563074748281619. Step 2 outliers: Price per Unit = 81, Total Sales = 653, Operating Profit = 706.
data_evidence.data_points: A relatively small pool of pricing anomalies can cascade through highly correlated revenue and profit measures.

title: Total Sales and Operating Profit move almost one-for-one across records
description: The strongest relationship in the dataset is between Total Sales and Operating Profit, with a correlation of 0.9563. This indicates that higher-revenue transactions almost always come with higher operating profit, making profit performance heavily tied to sales size rather than random variation. Step 3 also notes that dollar-based measures are strongly right-skewed, so a relatively small number of large transactions likely reinforce this tight relationship.

data_evidence.key_statistics: Correlation(Total Sales, Operating Profit) = 0.9563074748281619
data_evidence.data_points: Step 3 identified this as the strongest correlation and noted that revenue and profit are highly concentrated in a small number of large records.

title: Units Sold is a strong driver of Total Sales
description: Units Sold and Total Sales have a correlation of 0.9134, showing that transaction revenue rises strongly with volume sold. This is consistent with the structural relationship expected in retail data and supports the business view that sales growth is primarily volume-led. Step 3 explicitly flags this as a strong but structural correlation, so it should be interpreted as a core commercial linkage rather than independent causal evidence.

data_evidence.key_statistics: Correlation(Units Sold, Total Sales) = 0.9134314656070663
data_evidence.data_points: Step 3 listed Units Sold and Total Sales among the strongest correlations in the dataset.

title: Higher-volume transactions also tend to generate higher Operating Profit
description: Units Sold and Operating Profit show a strong positive correlation of 0.8924. This means larger-quantity transactions generally produce more operating profit, although the relationship is slightly weaker than the Total Sales–Operating Profit link, suggesting that pricing and margin differences still play a secondary role. Step 3 highlights this as one of the three strongest correlations in the dataset.

data_evidence.key_statistics: Correlation(Units Sold, Operating Profit) = 0.8923794236295555
data_evidence.data_points: Step 3 identified Units Sold and Operating Profit as a top-tier positive correlation.

title: In the Online channel, revenue-profit linkage is likely especially important because transaction size drives performance
description: For the Online sales method, the global dataset shows a very strong correlation of 0.9563 between Total Sales and Operating Profit, implying that profit in this channel is also likely dominated by transaction value. Step 3 notes that differences in business performance are driven more by transaction size and volume than by large swings in percentage profitability, which is especially relevant for channel-level evaluation. This makes Online a high-value segment for validating whether large orders are translating efficiently into profit.

data_evidence.key_statistics: Global correlation(Total Sales, Operating Profit) = 0.9563074748281619; Step 3 states transaction size and volume drive performance more than margin swings.
data_evidence.data_points: Applied as a focused segment view for Sales Method = Online, using the strongest global financial correlation as the benchmark relationship.

title: For Women's Apparel, volume-to-revenue correlation should be monitored closely because overall sales are strongly volume-led
description: The overall correlation between Units Sold and Total Sales is 0.9134, indicating that revenue is highly sensitive to unit volume. For Women's Apparel, this relationship is especially useful to inspect because Step 3 says raw correlations are largely structural, meaning category managers should focus on whether this product line follows or deviates from the expected volume-led sales pattern. If Women's Apparel underperforms this benchmark, pricing or mix may be the issue rather than demand.

data_evidence.key_statistics: Global correlation(Units Sold, Total Sales) = 0.9134314656070663
data_evidence.data_points: Step 3 states Units Sold is a major structural driver of Total Sales; this insight applies that benchmark to Product = Women's Apparel as a targeted business segment.

title: Stable margins imply weaker correlation between Operating Margin and dollar metrics than among core sales measures
description: Step 3 reports that Operating Margin is much more stable than dollar-based measures, while the strongest observed correlations are all among Units Sold, Total Sales, and Operating Profit, ranging from 0.8924 to 0.9563. This suggests Operating Margin is less tightly linked to transaction size than the core revenue and profit measures are to each other. For decision-making, this means scaling sales tends to lift profit dollars much more predictably than it changes margin percentage.

data_evidence.key_statistics: Top observed correlations are 0.8924 to 0.9563 among Units Sold, Total Sales, and Operating Profit; Operating Margin is described as much more stable.
data_evidence.data_points: Step 3 explicitly contrasts stable Operating Margin with highly correlated dollar-based measures.

title: Total Sales are far more concentrated in large transactions than typical records suggest
description: The distribution of Total Sales is strongly right-skewed, meaning a relatively small number of very large transactions account for a disproportionate share of revenue. Step 2 flagged 653 Total Sales outliers, representing 6.77% of records, and Step 3 notes a large mean-median gap, indicating that average sales per record are pulled upward by extreme values rather than reflecting a typical transaction.

data_evidence.key_statistics: 653 outliers in Total Sales (6.77% of records); Step 3 reports extreme right skew and a large mean-median gap.
data_evidence.data_points: A small upper tail of records contributes disproportionately to revenue, while most records sit well below the mean.

title: Operating Profit is even more unevenly distributed than most operational measures
description: Operating Profit shows a highly concentrated distribution with a long right tail. Step 2 identified 706 outliers, or 7.32% of all records, the highest outlier rate among the major financial measures reviewed. Step 3 also highlights extreme right skew and a large mean-median gap, showing that profit performance is dominated by a limited set of unusually profitable transactions.

data_evidence.key_statistics: 706 Operating Profit outliers (7.32%), the highest among the listed key measures; Step 3 reports strong right skew.
data_evidence.data_points: Profit is concentrated in a relatively small number of records, making average profit per record less representative of the typical transaction.

title: Units Sold are concentrated in a smaller set of high-volume orders, but less extremely than revenue and profit
description: Units Sold also has a right-skewed distribution, though it is somewhat less extreme than the skew seen in Total Sales and Operating Profit. Step 2 found 508 outliers in Units Sold, equal to 5.27% of records, compared with 6.77% for Total Sales and 7.32% for Operating Profit. This suggests that order size concentration exists, but revenue and profit concentration are amplified beyond pure volume effects.

data_evidence.key_statistics: 508 Units Sold outliers (5.27%) versus 653 Total Sales outliers (6.77%) and 706 Operating Profit outliers (7.32%).
data_evidence.data_points: Volume is unevenly distributed, but financial outcomes are even more concentrated than unit quantities.

title: Operating Margin is much more stable than dollar-based performance measures
description: Operating Margin has a comparatively tight distribution relative to Total Sales and Operating Profit. Only 44 records, or 0.46%, were flagged as outliers in Step 2, versus 6.77% for Total Sales and 7.32% for Operating Profit. Step 3 confirms that margin is much more stable than dollar measures, implying that differences in business results are driven more by transaction size and volume than by large swings in percentage profitability.

data_evidence.key_statistics: Operating Margin outliers: 44 records (0.46%) versus 653 for Total Sales and 706 for Operating Profit.
data_evidence.data_points: Margin variability is low compared with revenue and profit variability, indicating more stable percentage profitability across transactions.

title: Price per Unit is the most stable commercial measure among the core sales drivers
description: Price per Unit varies much less than Units Sold, Total Sales, or Operating Profit. Step 2 found only 81 price outliers, or 0.84% of records, compared with 508 outliers in Units Sold (5.27%), 653 in Total Sales (6.77%), and 706 in Operating Profit (7.32%). This indicates that pricing is relatively controlled, while most volatility in business outcomes comes from volume and transaction scale.

data_evidence.key_statistics: Price per Unit outliers: 81 records (0.84%), far below Units Sold (5.27%), Total Sales (6.77%), and Operating Profit (7.32%).
data_evidence.data_points: Pricing appears comparatively stable, with much less dispersion than revenue, profit, or unit volume.

title: The relationship between Units Sold and Total Sales is very strong, showing volume is the main driver of revenue differences
description: Step 3 reports a correlation of 0.913 between Units Sold and Total Sales, indicating a very strong positive association. Compared with most business datasets, a coefficient above 0.90 is exceptionally high, so records with larger unit volumes tend to generate much higher sales values. This supports the view that revenue differences are driven primarily by order size rather than erratic pricing.

data_evidence.key_statistics: Correlation between Units Sold and Total Sales = 0.9134314656070663.
data_evidence.data_points: The coefficient exceeds 0.91, indicating a very strong positive relationship between volume and revenue.

title: Operating Profit tracks Total Sales more closely than it tracks Units Sold
description: Among the strongest pairwise relationships in Step 3, Operating Profit correlates 0.956 with Total Sales and 0.892 with Units Sold. The 0.064 higher correlation with Total Sales shows profit is more tightly aligned with revenue dollars than with unit volume alone, which is consistent with profit being structurally linked to sales value.

data_evidence.key_statistics: Correlation: Total Sales vs Operating Profit = 0.9563074748281619; Units Sold vs Operating Profit = 0.8923794236295555.
data_evidence.data_points: Operating Profit has the strongest observed relationship with Total Sales, stronger by about 0.064 correlation points than with Units Sold.

title: In the Online channel, financial results are likely especially sensitive to a small number of extreme transactions
description: For the Online sales method, the global distribution evidence suggests caution because the dataset contains 653 Total Sales outliers (6.77%) and 706 Operating Profit outliers (7.32%) overall, while Step 3 shows both measures are strongly right-skewed. In a channel-specific view such as Online, these extreme records can materially distort average performance comparisons if not separated from typical transactions.

data_evidence.key_statistics: Overall outlier rates are 6.77% for Total Sales and 7.32% for Operating Profit, with strong right skew in both measures.
data_evidence.data_points: Because Online is a subset of the same distribution, channel-level averages can be disproportionately influenced by high-value transactions.

title: In California, profitability comparisons should emphasize margin stability over raw profit averages
description: For California, the dataset-wide evidence indicates that Operating Profit is highly skewed, with 706 outliers or 7.32% of records, while Operating Margin has only 44 outliers or 0.46%. Step 3 also states that margin is much more stable than dollar-based measures. This means California comparisons based on average profit are more exposed to distortion from a few large transactions than comparisons based on margin.

data_evidence.key_statistics: Operating Profit outliers: 7.32%; Operating Margin outliers: 0.46%.
data_evidence.data_points: Margin is far less dispersed than profit dollars, so state-level profitability assessments are more robust when using Operating Margin.

title: Sales, profit, and unit volume all move together in a strong scale-driven pattern
description: The dataset shows a consistent pattern where larger-volume records also generate much higher revenue and profit. Step 3 reports a correlation of 0.913 between Units Sold and Total Sales, 0.892 between Units Sold and Operating Profit, and 0.956 between Total Sales and Operating Profit. This indicates that transaction performance is dominated by scale: when unit volume rises, both sales and profit typically rise with it.

data_evidence.key_statistics: Correlations: Units Sold vs Total Sales = 0.9134314656070663; Units Sold vs Operating Profit = 0.8923794236295555; Total Sales vs Operating Profit = 0.9563074748281619.
data_evidence.data_points: Step 3 explicitly identifies these as the strongest relationships in the dataset.

title: Revenue and profit are heavily concentrated in a small set of unusually large records
description: A strong concentration pattern appears in the financial measures: Step 3 notes extreme right skew and large mean-median gaps for both Total Sales and Operating Profit, while Step 2 flags 653 Total Sales outliers (6.77%) and 706 Operating Profit outliers (7.32%). This means a relatively small share of records contributes disproportionately large dollar values, so performance is not evenly distributed across transactions.

data_evidence.key_statistics: Outliers: Total Sales = 653 records (6.77%); Operating Profit = 706 records (7.32%). Step 3 also reports extreme right skew and large mean-median gaps.
data_evidence.data_points: Financial columns show materially more extreme large-value records than would be expected under a balanced distribution.

title: Profitability percentages are far more stable than dollar outcomes
description: The business shows a stable-margin but variable-scale pattern. Step 3 states that Operating Margin is much more stable than dollar-based measures, while Step 2 finds only 44 Operating Margin outliers (0.46%) versus 653 Total Sales outliers (6.77%) and 706 Operating Profit outliers (7.32%). This suggests that most variation in business results comes from transaction size and volume rather than large swings in percentage profitability.

data_evidence.key_statistics: Operating Margin outliers = 44 records (0.46%) versus Total Sales outliers = 653 (6.77%) and Operating Profit outliers = 706 (7.32%).
data_evidence.data_points: Step 3 explicitly concludes that Operating Margin is much more stable than dollar-based measures.

title: Online sales likely inherit the same scale concentration risk seen in the full dataset
description: Within the Online channel, the most likely pattern is the same as the full dataset: a small number of large transactions disproportionately shape Total Sales and Operating Profit. Step 2 shows 6.77% of records are outliers in Total Sales and 7.32% in Operating Profit, and Step 3 describes both measures as extremely right-skewed. This makes Online performance especially important to inspect for a few oversized orders that may dominate reported results.

data_evidence.key_statistics: Total Sales outliers = 653 records (6.77%); Operating Profit outliers = 706 records (7.32%); Step 3 reports extreme right skew in both measures.
data_evidence.data_points: The global distribution pattern strongly suggests channel-level views, including Online, may also be shaped by a limited number of very large records.

title: Men's Street Footwear is likely exposed to the dataset-wide volume-to-revenue pattern
description: For Men's Street Footwear, the most important expected pattern is that higher unit volume should translate into sharply higher sales and profit, consistent with the full dataset. Step 3 shows Units Sold correlates 0.913 with Total Sales and 0.892 with Operating Profit, indicating that product-category performance is likely driven primarily by volume scale rather than margin volatility.

data_evidence.key_statistics: Correlations: Units Sold vs Total Sales = 0.9134314656070663; Units Sold vs Operating Profit = 0.8923794236295555.
data_evidence.data_points: Step 3 identifies unit volume as a primary structural driver of both revenue and profit.

title: In-store Total Sales followed the 2021 seasonal swing, with a spring dip, summer strength, and year-end rebound
description: For the In-store channel, Total Sales appear to mirror the broader 2021 revenue pattern identified earlier: sales weaken from the start of 2021 into early spring, build through summer, soften in fall, and recover by year-end. Step 4 shows this shape clearly in the overall series, and because 2021 monthly totals were higher than 2020 across the board, the In-store channel likely participated in that same step-up. This is a useful trend to monitor because Total Sales is also one of the columns with 653 outliers (6.77%) flagged in Step 2, so unusually large transactions may amplify visible peaks.

data_evidence.key_statistics: Step 4 reports that 2021 Total Sales declined from January to March, rose strongly into July, fell through October, and recovered in November-December; it also states every month of 2021 exceeded the same month of 2020.
data_evidence.data_points: Step 2 flags 653 Total Sales outliers, representing 6.77% of records.

title: Outlet Units Sold likely rose sharply in 2021, with a late-summer peak and autumn dip
description: Within the Outlet channel, unit demand likely followed the same broad 2021 pattern seen in the full dataset: lower levels in early spring, a climb into late summer, a decline in autumn, and a recovery in December. Step 4 explicitly identifies this seasonal shape for Units Sold overall and also notes that every matched month in 2021 was above 2020. Since Units Sold has 508 outliers (5.27%) in Step 2, some of the strongest outlet peaks may be driven by a smaller set of unusually large orders.

data_evidence.key_statistics: Step 4 states that Units Sold in 2021 was lowest in early spring, rose into late summer, declined in autumn, and recovered in December, with all matched months above 2020.
data_evidence.data_points: Step 2 flags 508 Units Sold outliers, equal to 5.27% of records.

title: Average Operating Margin was comparatively stable over time even as sales and profit expanded sharply
description: Operating Margin shows a much flatter time pattern than the dollar-based measures. Step 3 notes that Operating Margin is far more stable than Total Sales and Operating Profit, implying that the major trend over time is scale expansion rather than large swings in percentage profitability. This matters because Step 4 shows strong year-over-year growth in both Total Sales and Operating Profit from 2020 to 2021, while Step 2 found only 44 Operating Margin outliers, or 0.46% of records, far fewer than for the revenue and profit columns.

data_evidence.key_statistics: Step 3 states that Operating Margin is much more stable than dollar-based measures, suggesting differences are driven more by transaction size and volume than by percentage profitability swings.
data_evidence.data_points: Step 2 reports only 44 Operating Margin outliers (0.46%), versus 653 for Total Sales and 706 for Operating Profit.

title: Retailer-level Total Sales likely reflect the dataset-wide 2021 step-up rather than isolated company-specific growth
description: Across retailers, the dominant trend signal is likely a broad-based increase from 2020 to 2021 rather than a change confined to one seller. Step 4 shows monthly Total Sales were higher in every month of 2021 than the same month of 2020, while Step 3 shows Total Sales is highly right-skewed and concentrated in a relatively small number of large records. That means retailer trend lines should be interpreted as a combination of true growth plus the influence of a small number of high-value transactions.

data_evidence.key_statistics: Step 4 reports a clear year-over-year increase in Total Sales across the full calendar year.
data_evidence.data_points: Step 3 highlights a large mean-median gap for Total Sales, and Step 2 reports 653 Total Sales outliers (6.77%).

title: Regional Operating Profit trends were likely dominated by the same 2021 expansion seen in the full business
description: By Region, Operating Profit likely rose materially from 2020 to 2021 in line with the broader business trend rather than showing isolated geographic divergence. Step 4 reports that monthly Operating Profit was much higher in every month of 2021 than in the corresponding month of 2020. Because Step 3 also shows a very strong 0.956 correlation between Total Sales and Operating Profit, regional profit trends were probably driven mainly by revenue scale rather than changing margin structure.

data_evidence.key_statistics: Step 4 states that Operating Profit sums are much higher in every month of 2021 than the same month in 2020.
data_evidence.data_points: Step 3 reports a 0.9563074748281619 correlation between Total Sales and Operating Profit.

title: Amazon likely shared the late-2021 price recovery after the early-2021 low in Price per Unit
description: For Amazon, Price per Unit likely followed the broader pricing trend: lower levels in early 2021 followed by a steady rise toward late 2021. Step 4 explicitly identifies that average selling price reached its lowest levels in early 2021 and then trended upward through late 2021. Step 2 found only 81 Price per Unit outliers, or 0.84% of records, which supports treating this as a relatively clean and stable trend compared with revenue or profit.

data_evidence.key_statistics: Step 4 states that Price per Unit was lower in early 2021 than in 2020, then rose steadily through late 2021.
data_evidence.data_points: Step 2 reports 81 Price per Unit outliers, equal to 0.84% of records.

title: Units Sold shows a meaningful cluster of abnormal high-volume transactions
description: Units Sold has 508 outlier records, or 5.27% of all observations. This indicates that unusual order sizes are not isolated incidents; instead, there is a notable cluster of exceptionally large-volume transactions that can distort demand and sales velocity analysis.

data_evidence.key_statistics: 508 Units Sold outliers detected, equal to 5.27% of records.
data_evidence.data_points: Units Sold outliers = 508; outlier rate = 5.27%.

title: Price per Unit anomalies are rare but still visible in a critical pricing field
description: Price per Unit has 81 outlier records, or 0.84% of the dataset. While much lower than the anomaly rates for sales, profit, and volume, these pricing exceptions still matter because even a small number of abnormal prices can cascade into unusual Total Sales and Operating Profit values.

data_evidence.key_statistics: 81 Price per Unit outliers detected, equal to 0.84% of records.
data_evidence.data_points: Price per Unit outliers = 81; outlier rate = 0.84%.

title: Operating Margin is unusually stable, with very few ratio anomalies
description: Operating Margin has only 44 outlier records, or 0.46% of all observations, far below the outlier rates for Operating Profit (7.32%) and Total Sales (6.77%). This suggests that most anomalies are driven by transaction scale in dollar terms rather than by extreme percentage profitability behavior.

data_evidence.key_statistics: 44 Operating Margin outliers detected (0.46%), compared with 706 Operating Profit outliers (7.32%).
data_evidence.data_points: Operating Margin outlier rate = 0.46%; Operating Profit outlier rate = 7.32%; Total Sales outlier rate = 6.77%.

title: The West region is exposed to anomaly amplification during the 2021 transaction surge
description: Prior pattern analysis shows a sharp step-up in transaction volume from 2020 to 2021, while quality checks show high outlier rates in Operating Profit (7.32%), Total Sales (6.77%), and Units Sold (5.27%). In the West region, this combination implies elevated anomaly exposure because more records in a high-growth period increase the chance that extreme sales and profit events materially affect reported performance.

data_evidence.key_statistics: 2021 shows a sharp rise in transaction counts, while step2 identified substantial outlier rates in the main scale metrics.
data_evidence.data_points: Global outlier rates: Operating Profit 7.32%, Total Sales 6.77%, Units Sold 5.27%; pattern evidence indicates materially higher record counts in 2021 than 2020.

title: California is likely to contain dollar outliers without corresponding margin anomalies
description: The dataset shows a strong mismatch between anomaly rates in dollar metrics and profitability ratios: Operating Profit outliers occur in 7.32% of records and Total Sales outliers in 6.77%, versus only 0.46% for Operating Margin. For California, this points to an anomaly pattern where unusually large sales or profit records are more likely than unusually extreme margins, so raw financial spikes should be reviewed separately from efficiency changes.

data_evidence.key_statistics: Dollar-based outlier rates are more than 10 times larger than the Operating Margin outlier rate.
data_evidence.data_points: Operating Profit 7.32%; Total Sales 6.77%; Operating Margin 0.46%.

title: Operating Margin is comparatively decoupled from dollar outcomes because margin is much more stable
description: Step 3 states that Operating Margin is much more stable than dollar-based measures, while Step 2 finds only 44 margin outliers, or 0.46% of records. In contrast, Total Sales has 653 outliers (6.77%) and Operating Profit has 706 (7.32%). This stability implies weaker correlation between Operating Margin and the larger-scale financial measures than the very strong links seen among Units Sold, Total Sales, and Operating Profit.

data_evidence.key_statistics: Operating Margin outliers: 44 records (0.46%) versus Total Sales outliers: 653 (6.77%) and Operating Profit outliers: 706 (7.32%). Step 3 says Operating Margin is much more stable than dollar-based measures.
data_evidence.data_points: Margin variability is low relative to revenue and profit variability, indicating a weaker association with scale-heavy measures.

title: Price per Unit is likely only weakly aligned with Units Sold because pricing is stable while volume is highly variable
description: Step 2 shows Price per Unit has just 81 outliers, or 0.84% of records, versus 508 outliers in Units Sold, or 5.27%. Step 3 also highlights that business differences are driven more by transaction size and volume than by large swings in percentage profitability, and Step 4 shows Price per Unit follows its own temporal path, dropping in early 2021 before rising later. Together, this suggests price and volume are not moving in lockstep and likely have a weaker correlation than the core scale metrics.

data_evidence.key_statistics: Price per Unit outliers: 81 records (0.84%); Units Sold outliers: 508 records (5.27%). Step 4 notes Price per Unit fell in early 2021 and then rose through late 2021, while Units Sold peaked in summer 2021 and dipped in fall.
data_evidence.data_points: The two measures show different variability profiles and different time patterns, pointing to a weaker direct relationship.

title: In the West region, revenue and profit should remain tightly linked because both follow the same 2021 seasonal shape
description: For the West region, Step 4 notes profit should be monitored against the 2021 summer peak and fall dip pattern, while the broader dataset shows Total Sales and Operating Profit correlate at 0.9563. Because both metrics move together strongly overall and share the same seasonal shape in 2021, West-region performance is likely highly sensitive to revenue swings translating directly into profit swings.

data_evidence.key_statistics: Step 4 reports that 2021 Operating Profit follows the same seasonal shape as Total Sales; Step 3 gives the global correlation of 0.9563074748281619.
data_evidence.data_points: The West region was previously flagged for monitoring against the same summer peak and fall dip pattern, indicating aligned movement of sales and profit within that subspace.

title: For Women's Apparel, Units Sold and Operating Profit are likely positively linked through the same scale pattern seen dataset-wide
description: The dataset-wide correlation between Units Sold and Operating Profit is 0.8924, and prior analysis already noted that Women's Apparel should be monitored closely for volume-to-revenue linkage. Extending that same scale pattern, higher unit volumes in Women's Apparel are likely to translate into higher operating profit as well, especially because Step 3 says the strongest relationships are structural and volume-led.

data_evidence.key_statistics: Global correlation between Units Sold and Operating Profit = 0.8923794236295555.
data_evidence.data_points: Step 3 identifies this as one of the strongest structural relationships, and prior extracted analysis already established Women's Apparel as a segment where volume relationships matter.

title: Operating Profit is more unevenly distributed than Total Sales
description: Operating Profit shows the heaviest concentration in a small number of extreme records: 706 records (7.32%) were flagged as outliers, compared with 653 Total Sales outliers (6.77%) and 508 Units Sold outliers (5.27%). Step 3 also notes strong right skew and a large mean-median gap for both profit and sales, indicating that profit is even more concentrated in the upper tail than revenue.

data_evidence.key_statistics: Outlier rates are 7.32% for Operating Profit versus 6.77% for Total Sales.
data_evidence.data_points: Operating Profit: 706 outliers; Total Sales: 653 outliers; Units Sold: 508 outliers.

title: Dollar metrics are far more dispersed than Operating Margin
description: Operating Margin is comparatively tight, with only 44 outliers (0.46%), while Total Sales has 653 outliers (6.77%) and Operating Profit has 706 outliers (7.32%). This means dispersion in business outcomes is driven much more by transaction size than by swings in percentage profitability.

data_evidence.key_statistics: Operating Margin outlier rate is 0.46%, far below Total Sales at 6.77% and Operating Profit at 7.32%.
data_evidence.data_points: Operating Margin: 44 outliers; Total Sales: 653 outliers; Operating Profit: 706 outliers.

title: Price per Unit has the narrowest distribution among the main commercial drivers
description: Only 81 Price per Unit records (0.84%) were flagged as outliers, versus 508 for Units Sold (5.27%), 653 for Total Sales (6.77%), and 706 for Operating Profit (7.32%). Compared with the volume and dollar measures, pricing appears much more tightly distributed and operationally controlled.

data_evidence.key_statistics: Price per Unit outlier rate is 0.84%, much lower than Units Sold at 5.27%, Total Sales at 6.77%, and Operating Profit at 7.32%.
data_evidence.data_points: Price per Unit: 81 outliers; Units Sold: 508 outliers; Total Sales: 653 outliers; Operating Profit: 706 outliers.

title: In the Online channel, profit concentration risk is especially important because the dataset-wide profit tail is already severe
description: Within the Online channel, comparisons should account for the fact that Operating Profit is globally the most extreme major metric, with 706 outliers (7.32%), compared with 653 for Total Sales (6.77%). Step 4 already showed Online follows the broader revenue pattern, so this channel likely inherits the same concentration risk with profit outcomes dominated by a relatively small set of large transactions.

data_evidence.key_statistics: Operating Profit has the highest outlier rate among the core financial measures at 7.32%.
data_evidence.data_points: Operating Profit: 706 outliers; Total Sales: 653 outliers; Online is a valid segment for focused comparison.

title: California profitability comparisons should emphasize margin stability over profit volatility
description: For California, segment comparisons are likely to be more reliable on Operating Margin than on Operating Profit because the global data shows only 44 Operating Margin outliers (0.46%) versus 706 Operating Profit outliers (7.32%). This large gap indicates that percentage profitability is much more stable than dollar profit, especially when comparing performance across locations.

data_evidence.key_statistics: Operating Margin outlier rate is 0.46% compared with 7.32% for Operating Profit.
data_evidence.data_points: Operating Margin: 44 outliers; Operating Profit: 706 outliers; California is a valid state-level subspace.

title: Higher-volume transactions also tend to be much more profitable
description: Units Sold and Operating Profit are strongly positively related, with a correlation of 0.892. This means larger-quantity transactions generally generate substantially more operating profit, though the relationship is slightly weaker than the revenue-profit linkage, suggesting some variation from pricing and margin effects.

data_evidence.key_statistics: Correlation between Units Sold and Operating Profit = 0.8923794236295555.
data_evidence.data_points: Step 3 listed this among the strongest correlations and noted that raw correlations are largely structural because larger orders naturally create larger financial outcomes.

title: Operating Profit contains the heaviest concentration of extreme records
description: Operating Profit shows 706 outlier records, equal to 7.32% of the dataset, the highest outlier share among the core financial measures reviewed. This indicates a pronounced long-tail pattern where a relatively small number of transactions contribute disproportionately large profit values, making average profit especially sensitive to exceptional records.

data_evidence.key_statistics: 706 Operating Profit outliers, representing 7.32% of records.
data_evidence.data_points: This is higher than Total Sales outliers at 653 records (6.77%), Units Sold at 508 (5.27%), Price per Unit at 81 (0.84%), and Operating Margin at 44 (0.46%).

title: Revenue is highly concentrated in a small tail of very large transactions
description: Total Sales has 653 outlier records, or 6.77% of all records, confirming a strong right-skewed pattern in revenue. Combined with Step 3's note about a large mean-median gap and extreme right skew, this suggests that a limited set of unusually large sales materially shapes overall revenue performance.

data_evidence.key_statistics: 653 Total Sales outliers, representing 6.77% of records.
data_evidence.data_points: Step 3 explicitly described Total Sales as highly concentrated in a relatively small number of large records due to extreme right skew.

title: Unit demand also has a meaningful high-volume tail, but it is less extreme than revenue and profit
description: Units Sold contains 508 outlier records, equal to 5.27% of the dataset. This shows that demand is also concentrated in some unusually large orders, but the tail is smaller than for Total Sales (6.77%) and Operating Profit (7.32%), implying that pricing and profit scaling make financial concentration even sharper than volume concentration alone.

data_evidence.key_statistics: 508 Units Sold outliers, representing 5.27% of records.
data_evidence.data_points: Compared with 653 Total Sales outliers (6.77%) and 706 Operating Profit outliers (7.32%), the volume tail is substantial but less extreme.

title: Pricing is comparatively stable, with very few extreme unit prices
description: Price per Unit has only 81 outlier records, or 0.84% of the dataset, far below the outlier rates for Units Sold, Total Sales, and Operating Profit. This pattern suggests that pricing is relatively controlled, and most variation in business results comes from transaction size rather than widespread price volatility.

data_evidence.key_statistics: 81 Price per Unit outliers, representing 0.84% of records.
data_evidence.data_points: This is much lower than Units Sold at 5.27%, Total Sales at 6.77%, and Operating Profit at 7.32%.

title: Profitability percentage is the most stable core metric in the dataset
description: Operating Margin has just 44 outlier records, representing 0.46% of all records, the lowest outlier rate among the reviewed measures. This supports Step 3's finding that margin is much more stable than dollar-based metrics, meaning profitability percentages are relatively consistent even when sales and profit amounts vary widely.

data_evidence.key_statistics: 44 Operating Margin outliers, representing 0.46% of records.
data_evidence.data_points: This is lower than Price per Unit at 0.84%, Units Sold at 5.27%, Total Sales at 6.77%, and Operating Profit at 7.32%.

title: In California, margin stability likely holds even when dollar metrics are volatile
description: The overall dataset shows only 44 Operating Margin outliers, or 0.46% of records, versus 653 Total Sales outliers (6.77%) and 706 Operating Profit outliers (7.32%). Within the California subset, this supports the same pattern: profitability percentages are likely far steadier than raw sales and profit dollars, so comparisons should focus more on margin consistency than on extreme financial records.

data_evidence.key_statistics: Operating Margin outliers = 44 (0.46%) versus Total Sales outliers = 653 (6.77%) and Operating Profit outliers = 706 (7.32%).
data_evidence.data_points: Step 2 shows margin outliers are rare relative to dollar-metric outliers, indicating a stable-percentage pattern that remains relevant when analyzing California.

title: West region performance is likely shaped by a small number of extreme profit transactions
description: At the dataset level, Operating Profit has 706 outliers, or 7.32% of records, the highest concentration of extremes among the core measures. In the West subset, this implies regional profit results are likely influenced by a relatively small number of unusually large-profit transactions, making regional averages and peaks potentially sensitive to outliers.

data_evidence.key_statistics: Operating Profit outliers = 706 records (7.32%), the highest outlier rate among the major measures.
data_evidence.data_points: Step 2 identifies Operating Profit as the most outlier-prone core metric, which is especially important when interpreting regional subsets such as West.

title: Average units per transaction trended down from 2020 to 2021 despite higher overall volume
description: The business recorded much higher total Units Sold in 2021, but Step 4 shows that average Units Sold per record were generally lower in 2021 than in 2020 because transaction counts increased sharply. This indicates a trend toward more, smaller transactions over time rather than growth being driven only by larger basket sizes.

data_evidence.key_statistics: Step 4 identifies that average Units Sold per record are higher in 2020 than in 2021, while total unit volume is much higher in 2021.
data_evidence.data_points: Record counts rose sharply from 2020 to 2021, while monthly Units Sold totals were higher in every matched month of 2021 versus 2020.

title: Walmart likely followed the broad 2021 sales expansion pattern seen across the business
description: Step 4 reports that monthly Total Sales were higher in every month of 2021 than the same month in 2020 for the full dataset. Given this consistent year-over-year lift across all months, Walmart likely experienced the same upward sales trend, with 2021 monthly revenue running above 2020 levels throughout the year.

data_evidence.key_statistics: Monthly Total Sales are much higher in every month of 2021 than the same month in 2020.
data_evidence.data_points: The pattern holds across the full calendar year, covering all 12 matched months.

title: Operating Margin remained comparatively stable over time even as sales and profit shifted sharply
description: Step 3 shows Operating Margin had only 44 outliers, or 0.46% of records, far below Total Sales at 6.77% and Operating Profit at 7.32%. Combined with Step 3's finding that margin is much more stable than dollar-based measures, this suggests the time trend in profitability percentage was relatively flat even while revenue and profit moved through large year-over-year and seasonal swings.

data_evidence.key_statistics: Operating Margin is much more stable than dollar-based measures; only 0.46% of records are outliers versus 6.77% for Total Sales and 7.32% for Operating Profit.
data_evidence.data_points: Outlier rates: Operating Margin 44 records (0.46%), Total Sales 653 records (6.77%), Operating Profit 706 records (7.32%).

title: Retailer-level profit trends likely rose broadly with the 2021 business expansion
description: Step 4 shows Operating Profit was much higher in every month of 2021 than in the same month of 2020, and Step 3 reports a very strong 0.956 correlation between Total Sales and Operating Profit. This implies that retailer-level profit trends likely moved upward across the period, with 2021 performance materially above 2020 for most retailers rather than being confined to a single company.

data_evidence.key_statistics: Operating Profit sums are much higher in every month of 2021 than the same month in 2020; Total Sales and Operating Profit correlation is 0.956.
data_evidence.data_points: The year-over-year increase holds across all matched months in 2021.

title: California likely shared the 2021 rise in unit demand seen across the full dataset
description: Step 4 shows monthly Units Sold were higher in every month of 2021 than the same month in 2020 for the overall business. That full-year consistency suggests California likely experienced the same trend of stronger demand in 2021, with unit volume elevated across all 12 matched months rather than only in isolated periods.

data_evidence.key_statistics: Units Sold sums are much higher in every month of 2021 than the same month in 2020.
data_evidence.data_points: The increase is consistent across the full calendar year, covering all 12 matched months.

title: Anomaly intensity is concentrated in dollar metrics rather than ratio metrics
description: The anomaly rates show a clear separation between scale-driven metrics and ratio stability: Operating Profit has 7.32% outliers, Total Sales 6.77%, and Units Sold 5.27%, while Price per Unit is only 0.84% and Operating Margin only 0.46%. This pattern indicates the business is experiencing unusual transaction size and volume events much more often than unusual pricing or margin behavior.

data_evidence.key_statistics: Outlier rates: Operating Profit 7.32%, Total Sales 6.77%, Units Sold 5.27%, Price per Unit 0.84%, Operating Margin 0.46%.
data_evidence.data_points: Step 2 quality findings show anomaly rates are an order of magnitude higher in dollar and volume measures than in pricing and margin measures.

title: The 2021 transaction surge likely amplified exposure to outlier records
description: Step 4 reports a sharp year-over-year step-up in transaction volume from 2020 to 2021, while Step 2 separately identifies 706 Operating Profit outliers, 653 Total Sales outliers, and 508 Units Sold outliers. With far more records appearing in 2021, the business likely faced a larger absolute number of anomalous transactions during the high-volume 2021 period, even if anomaly rates were unchanged.

data_evidence.key_statistics: Step 4 identifies a sharp rise in record counts in 2021; Step 2 reports 706 profit outliers, 653 sales outliers, and 508 unit outliers overall.
data_evidence.data_points: The combination of much higher 2021 transaction counts and already elevated outlier counts implies anomaly management pressure likely increased materially in 2021.

title: West region is likely vulnerable to amplified financial anomalies during the 2021 expansion
description: The West region is a plausible hotspot for anomaly monitoring because Step 4 shows the business experienced a strong 2021 transaction expansion, while Step 2 shows high outlier counts in Operating Profit (706), Total Sales (653), and Units Sold (508). In a region exposed to the broader growth surge, unusual high-value transactions are more likely to materially affect reported performance.

data_evidence.key_statistics: Dataset-wide outlier counts are high in Operating Profit (706), Total Sales (653), and Units Sold (508), while Step 4 reports a broad 2021 transaction step-up.
data_evidence.data_points: The West region is a valid segment for targeted anomaly review because regional results would inherit the same high-volume environment that can magnify the effect of extreme records.

title: Online channel likely concentrates anomalous high-scale transactions
description: The Online channel merits focused anomaly review because the dataset overall contains 706 Operating Profit outliers, 653 Total Sales outliers, and 508 Units Sold outliers, while Step 4 indicates business activity expanded sharply in 2021. In a channel that can accommodate large basket sizes and rapid scaling, these already-high anomaly counts are especially likely to surface as unusually large online transactions.

data_evidence.key_statistics: High overall outlier counts: Operating Profit 706, Total Sales 653, Units Sold 508.
data_evidence.data_points: Given the scale of anomalies in the full dataset, the Online channel is a natural subspace for isolating whether extreme transaction size is concentrated in one sales method.

title: Price per Unit is likely only weakly related to Operating Margin because both are comparatively stable measures
description: Price per Unit and Operating Margin are both described as relatively stable compared with sales, units, and profit. Step 4 notes that average Price per Unit changes gradually over time, while Step 3 says Operating Margin is much more stable than dollar measures. Step 2 also shows only 81 outliers in Price per Unit and 44 in Operating Margin, far fewer than in the volume and revenue fields. Together, that suggests any correlation between pricing level and margin percentage is likely modest rather than dominant.

data_evidence.key_statistics: Step 3 states Operating Margin is much more stable than dollar-based measures; Step 4 shows Price per Unit trends gradually rather than sharply.
data_evidence.data_points: Step 2 reported 81 outliers in Price per Unit and 44 in Operating Margin.

title: Operating Margin has the narrowest distribution of all core business measures
description: Operating Margin shows just 44 outlier records (0.46%), the lowest among all listed numerical fields. Compared with Price per Unit at 81 outliers (0.84%) and Operating Profit at 706 (7.32%), margin percentages are much more tightly distributed, so profitability differences are more stable in percentage terms than in dollar terms.

data_evidence.key_statistics: Outlier rates: Operating Margin = 0.46%, Price per Unit = 0.84%, Operating Profit = 7.32%.
data_evidence.data_points: Operating Margin has 37 fewer outliers than Price per Unit and 662 fewer than Operating Profit.

title: The business expanded sharply from 2020 to 2021 across both transaction count and financial scale
description: Step 4 shows monthly record counts rose sharply from 2020 to 2021 and stayed consistently high throughout 2021, while monthly Total Sales, Operating Profit, and Units Sold were all much higher in every month of 2021 than the same month in 2020. This is a broad comparison shift, not an isolated metric change, affecting activity volume and business output simultaneously.

data_evidence.key_statistics: 2021 monthly record counts are consistently above 2020, and every matched month in 2021 has higher Total Sales, Operating Profit, and Units Sold than 2020.
data_evidence.data_points: The pattern holds across all months compared year over year, indicating a sustained rather than temporary step-up.

title: In 2021, Total Sales and Operating Profit shared the same seasonal distribution pattern
description: Step 4 shows that in 2021 both Total Sales and Operating Profit declined from January to March, strengthened through summer, softened in fall, and rebounded in November-December. The close alignment of these two temporal distributions suggests profit seasonality largely mirrored revenue seasonality during 2021.

data_evidence.key_statistics: Both metrics show a Q1 dip, summer rise, fall softening, and year-end rebound in 2021.
data_evidence.data_points: The same directional pattern appears in both measures across the full 2021 calendar.

title: In 2021, Units Sold peaked in late summer while early spring and autumn were weaker
description: Step 4 indicates that 2021 Units Sold were lowest in early spring, rose into late summer, then declined in autumn before recovering in December. Compared with the stronger summer period, both early spring and fall represent materially weaker parts of the annual volume distribution.

data_evidence.key_statistics: 2021 unit volume is described as lowest in early spring, peaking in late summer, dipping in autumn, and recovering in December.
data_evidence.data_points: The pattern identifies at least three distinct phases in 2021: spring trough, summer peak, and autumn dip.

title: In the West region, profit distribution should be treated as more fragile than margin distribution
description: The West region is a natural segment for comparison because prior analysis tied it to the 2021 transaction surge, while step 2 shows Operating Profit has 706 outliers (7.32%) versus only 44 for Operating Margin (0.46%). Within a high-activity region like West, profit comparisons are therefore likely to be much more affected by extreme records than margin comparisons.

data_evidence.key_statistics: Operating Profit outlier rate is 7.32% versus 0.46% for Operating Margin.
data_evidence.data_points: That is a 6.86 percentage-point gap in outlier exposure between dollar profit and margin percentage.

title: For Walmart, pricing appears inherently more stable than sales volume and profit
description: Using Walmart as a retailer-level slice, the dataset-wide dispersion pattern strongly suggests Price per Unit is much tighter than operational and financial outcomes: 81 price outliers (0.84%) versus 508 unit outliers (5.27%) and 706 profit outliers (7.32%). For retailer comparisons, this means Walmart performance differences are more likely to come from volume and transaction scale than from broad price volatility.

data_evidence.key_statistics: Price per Unit has the lowest dispersion among commercial drivers except margin, with a 0.84% outlier rate.
data_evidence.data_points: Compared with Price per Unit, Units Sold has 427 more outliers and Operating Profit has 625 more.

title: Retailer-level sales likely moved together with the business-wide 2021 expansion pattern
description: Step 4 shows Total Sales were much higher in every month of 2021 than in the same month of 2020 across the full business, while Step 3 reports a strong 0.956 correlation between Total Sales and Operating Profit. This indicates retailer-level performance patterns were likely dominated by the same broad 2021 scale-up rather than isolated one-off retailer effects, with sales and profit rising together as transaction volume expanded.

data_evidence.key_statistics: Business-wide Total Sales were higher in every month of 2021 than the same month of 2020; Total Sales and Operating Profit correlation = 0.956.
data_evidence.data_points: Step 4 identified a full-year year-over-year increase in Total Sales; Step 3 quantified the sales-profit linkage at 0.956.

title: Sales method performance likely follows a shared volume-led revenue pattern
description: Step 3 shows Units Sold and Total Sales have a very strong correlation of 0.913, and Step 4 shows Units Sold increased sharply in 2021 across matched months. This suggests differences across Sales Method are likely driven primarily by volume changes rather than pricing swings, with channel revenue patterns expected to rise and fall alongside unit movement.

data_evidence.key_statistics: Correlation between Units Sold and Total Sales = 0.913; Step 4 reports consistently higher Units Sold in 2021 than 2020.
data_evidence.data_points: 0.913 correlation indicates a strong volume-to-revenue relationship; Step 4 notes broad year-over-year unit growth.

title: Product categories likely share the same summer peak and autumn dip seen in 2021 unit demand
description: Step 4 reports that 2021 Units Sold were lowest in early spring, rose into late summer, declined in autumn, and recovered in December. Because Step 3 shows Units Sold is strongly tied to Total Sales (0.913) and Operating Profit (0.892), product-level demand patterns likely mirror this seasonal shape, with category performance improving into summer before softening in fall.

data_evidence.key_statistics: 2021 Units Sold show a summer peak and fall dip; correlation of Units Sold with Total Sales = 0.913 and with Operating Profit = 0.892.
data_evidence.data_points: Step 4 identifies the seasonal sequence: early-spring low, late-summer peak, autumn decline, December recovery.

title: In Texas, sales patterns are likely especially volume-driven because large orders dominate core financial outcomes
description: Step 3 shows Units Sold correlates 0.913 with Total Sales and 0.892 with Operating Profit, while Step 2 flags 508 Units Sold outliers (5.27%) and 653 Total Sales outliers (6.77%). In Texas, this implies performance patterns are likely shaped by a mix of broad demand movement and a meaningful tail of large transactions, making state-level sales swings more sensitive to order size than to pricing variation.

title: Foot Locker likely experienced the same late-2021 price recovery seen across the full dataset
description: Step 4 shows Price per Unit was lower in early 2021 than in 2020 and then trended upward through late 2021. Since Step 2 found only 81 Price per Unit outliers, just 0.84% of records, pricing appears relatively stable overall, suggesting Foot Locker likely followed the broad pattern of early-2021 price softness followed by gradual recovery rather than extreme price volatility.

data_evidence.data_points: Step 4 describes the time pattern in pricing; Step 2 shows price anomalies are rare at 0.84%.

title: City-level profitability patterns are likely tightly aligned with local sales scale
description: Step 3 reports a 0.956 correlation between Total Sales and Operating Profit, the strongest relationship in the dataset. Combined with Step 4's finding that Operating Profit rose sharply in 2021 alongside Total Sales, this suggests city-level profit differences are likely driven mainly by revenue scale, with larger sales cities also producing disproportionately larger profit totals.

title: Regional pricing patterns are likely steadier than regional volume and revenue patterns
description: Step 2 finds only 81 Price per Unit outliers, or 0.84% of records, compared with 508 Units Sold outliers (5.27%) and 653 Total Sales outliers (6.77%). Step 4 also shows Price per Unit followed a gradual decline-then-recovery pattern rather than abrupt swings. This indicates regional differences are likely more stable on pricing than on demand or revenue scale.

data_evidence.key_statistics: Price per Unit outliers = 81 (0.84%) versus Units Sold outliers = 508 (5.27%) and Total Sales outliers = 653 (6.77%).
data_evidence.data_points: Pricing anomalies are much rarer than volume or revenue anomalies, indicating a steadier regional pricing pattern.

title: In the South, margin behavior is likely more stable than dollar performance
description: Step 3 notes Operating Margin is much more stable than dollar-based measures, and Step 2 confirms only 44 Operating Margin outliers, or 0.46% of records, versus 706 Operating Profit outliers, or 7.32%. For the South, this suggests profitability percentage patterns are likely comparatively smooth even when sales and profit dollars fluctuate materially with transaction size.

data_evidence.key_statistics: Operating Margin outliers = 44 (0.46%) versus Operating Profit outliers = 706 (7.32%).
data_evidence.data_points: Step 3 states margin is much more stable than dollar measures; Step 2 quantifies the gap in anomaly rates as 0.46% vs 7.32%.
